changes.result: HARDENING NOTE — STOP condition as written ('is missing') understates the risk; governance docs exist on disk but are UNTRACKED
changes.citation: git status --short output: '?? docs/governance/KNOWLEDGE_BUS_PASS3_PROMOTION_PROTOCOL_v1.0.md', '?? docs/governance/KNOWLEDGE_BUS_PASS3_PROMOTION_PROTOCOL_v1.1.md', '?? docs/governance/KNOWLEDGE_BUS_SOP_v1.3.1.md'. These files exist on disk so the STOP condition 'is missing' would NOT trigger. However the prompt baseline (lines 40-45) requires these to be 'committed'. Cursor must run 'git status --short' and STOP if either file appears as '??' — file present on disk but untracked does NOT satisfy the baseline requirement. Commit these docs to main before creating the sprint branch.
